description: Normalize invalid relationship AUTH1 provider payload handling so missing, malformed, unknown-version, truncated, or plan-ordinal-mismatched failure-set payloads fail closed as security-configuration errors instead of generic unknown/system failures or relationship 403 fallbacks.
acceptance-criteria: All invalid relationship AUTH1 payload cases return the canonical security-configuration ProblemDetails type `urn:ed-fi:api:system:configuration:security` with status 500.

The security-configuration `errors` array contains exactly `The relationship authorization failure payload returned by the authorization provider is invalid and cannot be mapped to the configured relationship authorization plan.` for every invalid relationship AUTH1 payload case.
Raw provider exception text, malformed payload fragments, parser reasons, unknown versions, and invalid ordinal details are not emitted on the wire.
Valid relationship AUTH1 denial payloads still map to relationship authorization 403 failures.
Non-AUTH1 database exceptions continue through the existing non-relationship exception path.
steps-to-verify: Review `RelationshipAuthorizationProviderFailureMapper`, `SingleRecordRelationshipAuthorizationExecutor`, `StoredRelationshipAuthorizationOrchestrator`, `RelationalWriteNoProfilePersister`, and repository result mapping for invalid-payload handling.

Run `dotnet test src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Plans.Tests.Unit/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Plans.Tests.Unit.csproj --filter "FullyQualifiedName~RelationshipAuthorizationAuth1FailurePayload"`.

Run `dotnet test src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Tests.Unit/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Tests.Unit.csproj --filter "FullyQualifiedName~SingleRecordRelationshipAuthorizationExecutor|FullyQualifiedName~RelationalDocumentStoreRepository"`.
Run `dotnet test src/dms/core/EdFi.DataManagementService.Core.Tests.Unit/EdFi.DataManagementService.Core.Tests.Unit.csproj --filter "FullyQualifiedName~Handler"`.

description: Harden relationship AUTH1 failure-set extraction and mapping so the versioned payload is treated as a failed OR group with plan-relative strategy and subject ordinals rather than as a single configured strategy index.
acceptance-criteria: A relationship AUTH1 payload can identify multiple failed OR strategies and multiple failed subjects per strategy.

Mapped `RelationshipAuthorizationFailure` preserves configured strategy index, relationship-local order, strategy name/kind, subject index, failure kind, auth object, root binding, securable element metadata, hints, and People subject metadata in deterministic configured strategy/subject/contributor order.
Mapped claim EducationOrganizationIds are normalized deterministically and do not contain duplicates.

Stored-value payloads reject proposed-value-missing subject failures, proposed-value payloads reject stored-value-null subject failures, and no-relationship subject failures remain valid for both value sources.
Payload strategy and subject ordinals that do not exist in the active relationship plan are treated as invalid payloads for the security-configuration path.

The empty EdOrg claims short-circuit builds a synthetic no-relationship failure DTO from all operation-applicable executable relationship OR strategies and subjects in configured order without composing AUTH1 SQL.
steps-to-verify: Review `RelationshipAuthorizationAuth1FailurePayloadCodec`, `RelationshipAuthorizationFailureMapper`, `RelationshipAuthorizationPlanner`, and the stored/proposed relationship authorization orchestrators.

Run `dotnet test src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Plans.Tests.Unit/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Plans.Tests.Unit.csproj --filter "FullyQualifiedName~RelationshipAuthorizationAuth1FailurePayload|FullyQualifiedName~RelationshipAuthorizationPlanner"`.

Run `dotnet test src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Tests.Unit/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Tests.Unit.csproj --filter "FullyQualifiedName~SingleRecordRelationshipAuthorizationExecutor"`.

description: Implement the relationship authorization ProblemDetails case selector and formatter that consumes the full `RelationshipAuthorizationFailure` DTO and produces the final RFC 9457 body for relationship 403 responses.
acceptance-criteria: Relationship denials produce ProblemDetails members `type`, `title`, `status`, `detail`, `errors`, and `correlationId` matching `auth.md`.
Mixed failed relationship OR groups use precedence order: stored-value invalid data or element uninitialized, then proposed-value element required, then no relationship established.
Lower-precedence failed entries do not hide or downgrade higher-precedence entries.
Stored-value null failures use type `urn:ed-fi:api:security:authorization:relationships:invalid-data:element-uninitialized` and existing-data wording.
Proposed-value missing failures use type `urn:ed-fi:api:security:authorization:relationships:access-denied:element-required`, proposed-data wording, and an empty `errors` array.
No-relationship and no-claims relationship failures use type `urn:ed-fi:api:security:authorization` and the EdOrg-claims wording, including `(none)` when the normalized claim list is empty.
When multiple stored-value invalid-data securable names are selected, `detail` uses the clarified plural existing-data sentence from the story answers.
When multiple proposed-value element-required securable names are selected, `detail` uses the clarified plural proposed-data sentence from the story answers.
No-relationship failures selected from multiple failed OR strategies emit one aggregated `errors` entry with de-duplicated readable names, not one error per failed strategy or subject.

Selected invalid-data failures emit one `errors` entry per configured relationship strategy entry in configured order, while proposed element-required failures preserve strategy identity internally without emitting strategy-specific wire errors.
No formatter path re-queries authorization state to build the response.
steps-to-verify: Review the relationship formatter implementation and confirm it is driven only by `RelationshipAuthorizationFailure`.
Run `dotnet test src/dms/core/EdFi.DataManagementService.Core.Tests.Unit/EdFi.DataManagementService.Core.Tests.Unit.csproj --filter "FullyQualifiedName~RelationshipAuthorizationProblemDetails"`.
Run `dotnet test src/dms/core/EdFi.DataManagementService.Core.Tests.Unit/EdFi.DataManagementService.Core.Tests.Unit.csproj --filter "FullyQualifiedName~FailureResponse"`.

description: Implement relationship ProblemDetails text helpers for readable securable names, singular/plural wording, EdOrg claim display, and authorization hint aggregation.
acceptance-criteria: MetaEd/readable securable element names carried by the relationship auth core are preferred over fallback JSON path derivation.
Fallback names derive from JSON paths such as `$.schoolReference.schoolId` as `SchoolId` when readable metadata is absent.
Single selected readable securable elements use the singular relationship error text.
Multiple selected readable securable elements use the plural relationship error text with de-duplicated display names in deterministic first-seen configured strategy/subject/contributor order.
A single EdOrg claim renders with `claim`; multiple EdOrg claims render with `claims`.
An empty normalized EdOrg claim list renders as `(none)` through the EdOrg-claims wording.
More than five EdOrg claims render only the first five deterministic values followed by `...`.

Distinct authorization hints from selected failed entries are appended to `detail` after the base detail text in deterministic configured strategy/subject order, including for invalid-data and proposed element-required failures.
Duplicate authorization hints are removed by hint text while preserving the first configured strategy/subject occurrence.
steps-to-verify: Run unit tests covering readable-name fallback, readable-name preference, singular and plural messages, no claims, one claim, five claims, more-than-five claims, duplicate display-name de-duplication, and duplicate hint de-duplication.
Run `dotnet test src/dms/core/EdFi.DataManagementService.Core.Tests.Unit/EdFi.DataManagementService.Core.Tests.Unit.csproj --filter "FullyQualifiedName~RelationshipAuthorizationProblemDetails"`.
Review the generated response strings against `reference/design/backend-redesign/design-docs/auth.md` section `ProblemDetails` and the clarifying answers in the story.

description: Wire the relationship ProblemDetails formatter into GET-by-id, DELETE, POST-create/POST-as-update, and PUT handler responses for relationship authorization failures while leaving non-relationship authorization responses on their existing path.
acceptance-criteria: `GetFailureRelationshipNotAuthorized`, `DeleteFailureRelationshipNotAuthorized`, `UpsertFailureRelationshipNotAuthorized`, and `UpdateFailureRelationshipNotAuthorized` use the new relationship formatter and the embedded `RelationshipAuthorizationFailure` DTO.
Legacy `FailureResponse.ForForbidden` behavior for non-relationship authorization failures remains unchanged except where shared infrastructure must support the new formatter.
Relationship failure responses use status 403 and `application/problem+json` where the surrounding handler convention requires a ProblemDetails content type.
`correlationId` is populated from the request trace id and remains dynamic; tests assert presence or an explicitly supplied echoed value rather than byte-for-byte fixed generated values.
Security-configuration failures continue to use status 500 and `FailureResponse.ForSecurityConfiguration`.
steps-to-verify: Review `GetByIdHandler`, `DeleteByIdHandler`, `UpsertHandler`, `UpdateByIdHandler`, and `FailureResponse` for relationship-specific routing.

Run `dotnet test src/dms/core/EdFi.DataManagementService.Core.Tests.Unit/EdFi.DataManagementService.Core.Tests.Unit.csproj --filter "FullyQualifiedName~GetByIdHandler|FullyQualifiedName~DeleteByIdHandler|FullyQualifiedName~UpsertHandler|FullyQualifiedName~UpdateByIdHandler"`.
Inspect handler unit assertions to confirm stable ProblemDetails members are exact and `correlationId` is validated as dynamic.

description: Add focused unit tests for relationship ProblemDetails formatting from full failed OR-strategy metadata, including mixed failure kinds, multiple strategies, People metadata, no-claims synthetic failures, and final wire text rules.
acceptance-criteria: Unit tests cover no-relationship formatting with one selected securable element and multiple selected securable elements.
Unit tests cover stored-value uninitialized existing-data formatting for one and multiple selected readable names.
Unit tests cover proposed-value missing proposed-data formatting for one and multiple selected readable names and assert `errors` is an empty array.
Unit tests cover mixed failure-kind precedence: stored null beats no relationship, proposed missing beats no relationship, and no relationship is selected only when no higher-precedence kind exists.
Unit tests cover multiple invalid-data strategy errors as one error entry per selected configured relationship strategy in configured order.
Unit tests cover no-relationship errors as a single aggregated error entry across selected failed OR strategies and subjects.
Unit tests cover People relationship DTO mapping, readable-name selection, People auth-view hints, and empty-claims formatting without requiring People CRUD endpoint execution.
steps-to-verify: Run `dotnet test src/dms/core/EdFi.DataManagementService.Core.Tests.Unit/EdFi.DataManagementService.Core.Tests.Unit.csproj --filter "FullyQualifiedName~RelationshipAuthorizationProblemDetails"`.

Run `dotnet test src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Plans.Tests.Unit/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Plans.Tests.Unit.csproj --filter "FullyQualifiedName~RelationshipAuthorizationAuth1FailurePayload"`.
Review unit test names for NUnit style: fixture classes prefixed with `Given_`, setup methods for arrange/act where appropriate, and test methods prefixed with `It_`.

description: Add PostgreSQL and SQL Server provider-level backend integration coverage that proves relationship failure DTOs and invalid-payload security-configuration paths are preserved for unauthorized stored-value operations.
acceptance-criteria: PostgreSQL GET-by-id unauthorized relationship denial preserves the full relationship failure DTO needed for final ProblemDetails formatting.
SQL Server GET-by-id unauthorized relationship denial preserves the full relationship failure DTO needed for final ProblemDetails formatting.
PostgreSQL DELETE unauthorized relationship denial preserves the full relationship failure DTO and still returns 403 before delete execution.
SQL Server DELETE unauthorized relationship denial preserves the full relationship failure DTO and still returns 403 before delete execution.
Stored null/uninitialized existing-data failures are covered for both dialects where fixture support exists.
Invalid relationship AUTH1 provider payload or unmappable payload ordinal cases surface as security-configuration failures rather than unknown failures.
Provider-level tests assert stable backend result contracts and DTO contents; final HTTP ProblemDetails body assertions are owned by the API-level integration task.
steps-to-verify: Run `dotnet test src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Postgresql.Tests.Integration/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Postgresql.Tests.Integration.csproj --filter "Category=Authorization"`.

Run `ConnectionStrings__MssqlAdmin='<admin-connection-string>' dotnet test src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Mssql.Tests.Integration/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Mssql.Tests.Integration.csproj --filter "Category=Authorization"` after verifying SQL Server is listening.
Review the PostgreSQL and SQL Server tests for equivalent assertions across dialects.

description: Add API-level integration tests for final HTTP ProblemDetails response bodies for unauthorized EdOrg relationship CRUD operations across PostgreSQL and SQL Server.
acceptance-criteria: PostgreSQL unauthorized GET-by-id response body matches the final relationship ProblemDetails contract for all stable members and validates `correlationId` as present and non-empty.
SQL Server unauthorized GET-by-id response body matches the final relationship ProblemDetails contract for all stable members and validates `correlationId` as present and non-empty.
PostgreSQL and SQL Server unauthorized POST-create response bodies match the final no-relationship and proposed element-required ProblemDetails contracts as applicable.
PostgreSQL and SQL Server unauthorized PUT response bodies distinguish stored-value invalid-data failures from proposed-value element-required failures.
PostgreSQL and SQL Server unauthorized DELETE response bodies match the final relationship ProblemDetails contract.
At least one API-level test validates empty EdOrg claims render through the EdOrg-claims wording as `(none)`.
Tests avoid dedicated custom-view/no-EdOrg-claims wire assertions because custom view ProblemDetails are out of scope for this story.
steps-to-verify: Run `dotnet test src/dms/tests/EdFi.DataManagementService.Tests.Integration/EdFi.DataManagementService.Tests.Integration.csproj --filter "Category=PostgresqlIntegration"`.

Run `ConnectionStrings__MssqlAdmin='<admin-connection-string>' dotnet test src/dms/tests/EdFi.DataManagementService.Tests.Integration/EdFi.DataManagementService.Tests.Integration.csproj --filter "Category=MssqlIntegration"` after verifying SQL Server is listening.
Inspect response-shape assertions to confirm stable members are exact and dynamic `correlationId` handling follows the story guidance.

description: Add focused relational-backend DMS E2E coverage for the externally observable relationship authorization ProblemDetails shape for one stored-value operation and one proposed-value operation.
acceptance-criteria: A stored-value relationship denial E2E scenario validates status 403, final relationship ProblemDetails `type`, `title`, `status`, `detail`, `errors`, and a present/non-empty `correlationId`.

A proposed-value relationship denial E2E scenario validates status 403, final proposed element-required ProblemDetails `type`, `title`, `status`, `detail`, empty `errors`, and a present/non-empty `correlationId`.
The scenarios run only under the relational backend and use the NUnit category `relational-backend`.
The E2E expected bodies do not pin a generated correlation id unless the scenario intentionally supplies the configured correlation-id request header and asserts it is echoed.
steps-to-verify: From `src/dms/tests/EdFi.DataManagementService.Tests.E2E`, run `pwsh ./teardown-local-dms.ps1`.
From `src/dms/tests/EdFi.DataManagementService.Tests.E2E`, run `pwsh ./setup-local-dms.ps1 -EnvironmentFile ./.env.e2e.relational`.
Verify relational backend settings with `docker inspect dms-local-dms-1 --format '{{range .Config.Env}}{{println .}}{{end}}' | rg 'UseRelationalBackend|Datastore|QueryHandler'`.

Run `env -u NODE_OPTIONS dotnet test EdFi.DataManagementService.Tests.E2E.csproj --configuration Release --filter "Category=relational-backend"` from `src/dms/tests/EdFi.DataManagementService.Tests.E2E`.

description: Protect out-of-scope authorization behavior while hardening relationship ProblemDetails so shared response infrastructure changes do not regress namespace, ownership, custom-view, GET-many filtering, or temporary staging behavior.
acceptance-criteria: Security-configuration failures from planning and invalid relationship AUTH1 payloads remain 500 responses and are not converted into 403 denials.
GET-many relationship authorization continues to filter rows and does not return 403 for unauthorized rows.
Namespace-based and ownership-based ProblemDetails behavior is unchanged except for shared helper changes required to preserve existing tests.
No new custom-view/no-EdOrg-claims ProblemDetails tests are added unless preserving existing shared formatter coverage requires them.
Temporary known-but-not-enabled staging surfaces for strategies outside the current CRUD relationship scope remain intact.
No database authorization objects, DDL, relationship strategy semantics, or People CRUD endpoint execution are introduced by this story.
steps-to-verify: Run `dotnet test src/dms/core/EdFi.DataManagementService.Core.Tests.Unit/EdFi.DataManagementService.Core.Tests.Unit.csproj --filter "FullyQualifiedName~ResourceActionAuthorizationMiddleware|FullyQualifiedName~QueryRequestHandler|FullyQualifiedName~FailureResponse"`.

Run `dotnet test src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Postgresql.Tests.Integration/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Postgresql.Tests.Integration.csproj --filter "FullyQualifiedName~PostgresqlRelationalQueryAuthorization"`.
Review the diff and confirm no DDL, auth object generation, strategy planner semantics outside failure DTO mapping, or People CRUD execution paths were added.

description: Run formatting and targeted verification for the full relationship ProblemDetails hardening change set.
acceptance-criteria: Changed C# files are formatted with CSharpier.
Core, backend unit, plans unit, and relevant backend/API integration tests pass for the changed surface.
Any skipped SQL Server integration tests are documented as environment skips, not treated as product signals.
The final review notes identify the exact tests run and any residual test gaps.
steps-to-verify: Run `dotnet csharpier format src/dms`.
Run `dotnet test src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Plans.Tests.Unit/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Plans.Tests.Unit.csproj`.
Run `dotnet test src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Tests.Unit/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Tests.Unit.csproj`.
Run `dotnet test src/dms/core/EdFi.DataManagementService.Core.Tests.Unit/EdFi.DataManagementService.Core.Tests.Unit.csproj`.
Run the relevant PostgreSQL and SQL Server integration commands from the backend-integration and api-integration tasks when the required databases are available.
Run the focused relational-backend E2E command from the e2e task after the relational dms-local stack is set up.

description: Medium: Limit relationship authorization ProblemDetails `Hint:` text to configured authorization-view hints instead of internal relationship failure diagnostics.
acceptance-criteria: Stored-value null, proposed-value missing, no-relationship, and no-claim failures do not append internal diagnostic text such as missing subject values, null stored values, provider row matching, or claim requirements as wire-visible `Hint:` text.
EdOrg-hierarchy-only relationship failures emit no hint unless a configured authorization object provides a user-facing failure hint.

People or view-backed relationship failures append only the configured user-facing authorization hint text from the selected auth object, without diagnostic prefixes such as the auth view name or claim requirement explanation.
Duplicate configured hint text is still de-duplicated while preserving deterministic configured strategy and subject order.
Core, API integration, and relational E2E expected ProblemDetails bodies are updated to match `auth.md` authorization hint rules.
steps-to-verify: Review `RelationshipAuthorizationFailureMapper`, `RelationshipAuthorizationPlanner`, and `RelationshipAuthorizationProblemDetails` for separation between internal failure diagnostics and wire-visible authorization hints.
Run `dotnet test src/dms/core/EdFi.DataManagementService.Core.Tests.Unit/EdFi.DataManagementService.Core.Tests.Unit.csproj --filter "FullyQualifiedName~RelationshipAuthorizationProblemDetails"`.
Run the relevant PostgreSQL and SQL Server API integration response-shape tests for relationship authorization ProblemDetails.
Run the focused relational-backend E2E scenarios after setting up the relational dms-local stack.

description: Medium: Validate the emitted AUTH1 check index from relationship authorization provider payloads against the operation's expected emitted check index.
acceptance-criteria: `RelationshipAuthorizationFailureMapper` rejects otherwise well-formed AUTH1 payloads whose emitted check index does not match the expected relationship authorization check for the current operation.
GET, DELETE, stored-value write checks, and proposed-value write checks all pass the expected emitted AUTH1 index into relationship failure mapping.
A mismatched emitted check index follows the canonical security-configuration failure path with the stable invalid-payload error message.
Valid payloads with the expected emitted check index continue to map to relationship authorization 403 failures.
Unit tests cover a well-formed payload with valid strategy and subject ordinals but an unexpected emitted AUTH1 index.
steps-to-verify: Review `RelationshipAuthorizationAuth1FailurePayload`, `RelationshipAuthorizationFailureMapper`, `SingleRecordRelationshipAuthorizationExecutor`, `RelationalWriteNoProfilePersister`, and repository provider-failure mapping call sites.

Run `dotnet test src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Plans.Tests.Unit/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Plans.Tests.Unit.csproj --filter "FullyQualifiedName~RelationshipAuthorizationAuth1FailurePayload"`.

Run `dotnet test src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Tests.Unit/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Tests.Unit.csproj --filter "FullyQualifiedName~SingleRecordRelationshipAuthorizationExecutor|FullyQualifiedName~RelationalDocumentStoreRepository"`.

description: Low: Remove or narrow transitional relationship authorization error-message formatting now that handlers render relationship ProblemDetails from `RelationshipAuthorizationFailure`.
acceptance-criteria: Relationship-specific backend result variants no longer carry unused `ErrorMessages` or `Hints` fields unless a current non-HTTP consumer still requires them.
`RelationshipAuthorizationErrorMessageFormatter` is removed, narrowed, or documented as backend-only if it still has an active consumer.
Stale TODO comments about future ProblemDetails hint rendering are removed because relationship ProblemDetails now owns wire hint rendering.
Backend unit tests assert relationship failure metadata instead of transitional parallel wire-text strings where possible.
HTTP handler behavior remains driven by `FailureResponse.ForRelationshipAuthorization(RelationshipAuthorizationFailure)`.
steps-to-verify: Review relationship-not-authorized result types in `EdFi.DataManagementService.Core.External/Backend` and their repository construction sites.
Review all references to `RelationshipAuthorizationErrorMessageFormatter` and remove or narrow any dead formatting path.
Run affected backend and core handler unit tests for relationship authorization failures.

description: Medium: Keep relationship authorization E2E ProblemDetails expectations aligned with the backend mode under test.
acceptance-criteria: Untagged/default-backend scenarios in `RelationshipsWithEdOrgsOnly.feature` do not expect relational-only `RelationshipAuthorizationFailure` ProblemDetails from the legacy non-relational authorization path.
Any scenario that asserts the new relational relationship ProblemDetails body is tagged with `@relational-backend` and an appropriate relational CI shard tag.

Existing default-backend relationship authorization coverage is preserved with expectations that match the non-relational `GetFailureNotAuthorized`/`UpsertFailureNotAuthorized` response path, unless the scenario is intentionally moved to relational-only coverage.
Focused final ProblemDetails shape coverage remains in relational-backend E2E scenarios and validates dynamic `correlationId` presence without pinning generated values.
Feature tags make the required DMS Docker environment unambiguous for both default and relational E2E runs.
steps-to-verify: Review `src/dms/tests/EdFi.DataManagementService.Tests.E2E/Features/Authorization/RelationshipsWithEdOrgsOnly.feature` for untagged scenarios that assert the new relationship ProblemDetails body.
Review `src/dms/tests/EdFi.DataManagementService.Tests.E2E/Features/Authorization/RelationshipsWithEdOrgsOnlyRelational.feature` for relational-only final ProblemDetails assertions.
Run the default E2E authorization scenarios against the default `.env.e2e` stack or inspect the generated NUnit categories to confirm untagged scenarios are not selected as relational-only tests.
Run the focused relational-backend E2E command after setting up the `.env.e2e.relational` stack.

description: Medium: Remove internal relationship authorization diagnostics from relational E2E ProblemDetails expectations.
acceptance-criteria: `RelationshipsWithEdOrgsOnly.feature` relational-backend 403 expectations do not append internal diagnostics such as `Relationship authorization requires at least one claim EducationOrganizationId.` or `No matching relationship authorization row was found for the subject value and claim EducationOrganizationIds.` as wire-visible `Hint:` text.
EdOrg-hierarchy-only no-claims and no-relationship denials expect the base detail `Access to the requested data could not be authorized.` unless a configured authorization-view failure hint applies.
Updated scenarios continue to assert the final relationship ProblemDetails `type`, `title`, `status`, `validationErrors`, and `errors` members.
Relational E2E scenarios that assert relationship ProblemDetails validate a present/non-empty dynamic `correlationId` with the dedicated step instead of pinning or ignoring generated values.
steps-to-verify: Review `src/dms/tests/EdFi.DataManagementService.Tests.E2E/Features/Authorization/RelationshipsWithEdOrgsOnly.feature` for stale internal diagnostic `Hint:` text in expected 403 bodies.
Review `src/dms/core/EdFi.DataManagementService.Core/Response/RelationshipAuthorizationProblemDetails.cs` to confirm only configured authorization-view hints are emitted.
From `src/dms/tests/EdFi.DataManagementService.Tests.E2E`, run the focused relational-backend E2E command after setting up the `.env.e2e.relational` stack.

description: High: Reject relationship AUTH1 failure payloads that omit a failed OR strategy from the active relationship plan.
acceptance-criteria: `RelationshipAuthorizationFailureMapper.TryMapAuth1Failure` treats a provider payload as invalid unless every relationship check spec in the failed OR group has at least one mapped failed subject.

A syntactically valid payload that contains failures for only a subset of configured relationship OR strategies follows the canonical security-configuration failure path instead of producing a relationship 403.

Valid payloads generated by `SingleRecordRelationshipAuthorizationSqlCompiler` for failed OR groups continue to map to relationship authorization failures with all failed strategies in deterministic order.
Unit tests cover an otherwise valid payload that omits one configured strategy ordinal when multiple relationship strategies are active.
steps-to-verify: Review `src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Plans/RelationshipAuthorizationFailureMapper.cs` around `TryMapAuth1Failure` and confirm omitted strategy ordinals are rejected.

Run `dotnet test src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Plans.Tests.Unit/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Plans.Tests.Unit.csproj --filter "FullyQualifiedName~RelationshipAuthorizationAuth1FailurePayload"`.

Run `dotnet test src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Tests.Unit/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Tests.Unit.csproj --filter "FullyQualifiedName~SingleRecordRelationshipAuthorizationExecutor"`.

description: High: Return security-configuration failures for proposed relationship authorization invalid-plan extraction.
acceptance-criteria: `ProposedRelationshipAuthorizationOrchestrator` maps `ProposedRelationshipAuthorizationExtractionResult.InvalidAuthorizationPlan` through `RelationalWriteExecutorResults.BuildSecurityConfigurationFailureResult` instead of `BuildUnknownFailureResult`.
POST and PUT proposed-value invalid authorization plans return the canonical security-configuration ProblemDetails type `urn:ed-fi:api:system:configuration:security` with status 500.
The security-configuration `errors` array contains the invalid-plan failure message and does not use the generic unknown/system-error response path.
Valid proposed relationship authorization extraction and authorization failures continue to return the existing relationship authorization 403 or successful write behavior.
steps-to-verify: Review `src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend/ProposedRelationshipAuthorizationOrchestrator.cs` and confirm the invalid-plan branch uses `BuildSecurityConfigurationFailureResult`.

Review `src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend/RelationalWriteExecutorResults.cs` and handler result mapping to confirm POST and PUT security-configuration variants render canonical ProblemDetails.
Run affected backend unit tests for proposed relationship authorization invalid-plan extraction and write executor result mapping.
Run affected core handler unit tests for `UpsertFailureSecurityConfiguration` and `UpdateFailureSecurityConfiguration`.

description: Low: Remove the unused external no-claim relationship authorization failure kind.
acceptance-criteria: `RelationshipAuthorizationSubjectFailureKind.NoClaimEducationOrganizationIds` is removed from the external `RelationshipAuthorizationFailure` DTO contract.

`RelationshipAuthorizationProblemDetails` no longer accepts a distinct external no-claim subject failure kind; no-claims relationship failures continue to format through `NoRelationship` with claims rendered as `(none)`.
`RelationshipAuthorizationFailureMapper.TryMapNoClaimsFailure` remains the only no-claims DTO construction path and continues to emit `NoRelationship` subjects for the formatter.
Unit tests and helper builders no longer construct the removed external failure kind.
steps-to-verify: Review `src/dms/core/EdFi.DataManagementService.Core.External/Backend/RelationshipAuthorizationFailure.cs` and confirm the external enum no longer contains `NoClaimEducationOrganizationIds`.
Review `src/dms/core/EdFi.DataManagementService.Core/Response/RelationshipAuthorizationProblemDetails.cs` and confirm the no-relationship case only selects `NoRelationship` failures.

Run `rg "RelationshipAuthorizationSubjectFailureKind\.NoClaimEducationOrganizationIds|NoClaimEducationOrganizationIds" src/dms/core src/dms/backend` and confirm remaining uses are limited to planner-internal `RelationshipAuthorizationFailureKind.NoClaimEducationOrganizationIds` where appropriate.
Run `dotnet test src/dms/core/EdFi.DataManagementService.Core.Tests.Unit/EdFi.DataManagementService.Core.Tests.Unit.csproj --filter "FullyQualifiedName~RelationshipAuthorizationProblemDetails"`.

description: Medium: Log invalid relationship AUTH1 provider payload diagnostics while keeping provider details off the wire.
acceptance-criteria: When relationship AUTH1 payload parsing or mapping fails, the backend logs enough diagnostic context to identify the dialect, expected emitted AUTH1 index, provider error code, provider message or payload fragment, and the mapping failure category.

The HTTP response continues to use only the stable security-configuration error `The relationship authorization failure payload returned by the authorization provider is invalid and cannot be mapped to the configured relationship authorization plan.`.
Raw provider text, malformed payload fragments, parser reasons, unknown versions, and invalid ordinal details are not included in ProblemDetails `detail` or `errors`.
Stored GET/DELETE authorization and proposed write authorization invalid-payload paths both emit the diagnostic log before returning or throwing the security-configuration failure.
steps-to-verify: Review `SingleRecordRelationshipAuthorizationExecutor`, `RelationalWriteNoProfilePersister`, and `DefaultRelationalWriteExecutor` for invalid relationship AUTH1 payload logging.
Run affected backend unit tests for invalid relationship AUTH1 payload handling.
Manually inspect a failing invalid-payload unit or integration run log to confirm provider diagnostics are present only in logs and not in the response body.
